task_completion.description: Navigate to the CAMEO Chemicals website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and displaying the CAMEO Chemicals homepage (cameochemicals.noaa.gov) or a search page on that domain.

task_completion.description: Search for and view the chemical data sheet for Sodium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the CAMEO Chemicals entry specifically for Sodium, showing its chemical profile and response recommendations.

task_completion.description: Search for and view the chemical data sheet for Acetone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the CAMEO Chemicals entry specifically for Acetone, showing its chemical profile and response recommendations.

task_completion.description: Search for and view the chemical data sheet for Magnesium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the CAMEO Chemicals entry specifically for Magnesium, showing its chemical profile and response recommendations.

task_completion.description: Create the fire response report file in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows a file named 'fire_response_report.txt' being created or edited in the '~/Documents' (or /home/ga/Documents) directory.

task_completion.description: Document the recommended fire extinguishing agents for all three chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'fire_response_report.txt' clearly list Sodium, Acetone, and Magnesium, along with specific fire extinguishing methods or agents for each.

task_completion.description: Identify water-reactive chemicals and include a summary statement prohibiting water use.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report explicitly identifies Sodium and Magnesium as water-reactive and contains a summary statement indicating that water must never be used on these specific chemicals.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the CAMEO Chemicals database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a graphical web browser (like Firefox or Chromium) navigating the CAMEO website, rather than relying on hallucinated knowledge or curl/wget commands in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to compile the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, mousepad, nano, vim) is visible on screen with the report contents, rather than the file being generated entirely via a single opaque terminal command (like echo or cat).

integrity.description: The agent did not apply a blanket rule to all chemicals but differentiated based on research.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report shows different extinguishing rules for Acetone (which allows water) versus Sodium/Magnesium (which prohibit water), proving the agent didn't just copy-paste a generic 'no water' warning for all three.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Sodium and Magnesium are water-reactive chemicals; their CAMEO profiles explicitly state 'DO NOT USE WATER' as they generate flammable hydrogen gas. Acetone is not water-reactive and its CAMEO profile permits the use of water spray or alcohol-resistant foam. The final report must be located at /home/ga/Documents/fire_response_report.txt and must correctly distinguish between the water-safe (Acetone) and water-reactive (Sodium, Magnesium) chemicals.
